history.summary: bash tool added with bubblewrap fs sandbox. buildSandboxArgs builds bwrap argv: --unshare-user/pid/ipc/uts/cgroup, --ro-bind /usr|/lib|/lib64|/bin|/sbin (read-only system libs so /bin/sh works), --bind workspaceRoot /workspace (only writable mount), --tmpfs /etc|/var|/tmp (so `cat /etc/passwd` finds nothing), --proc /proc, --dev /dev, --chdir /workspace, --cap-drop ALL, --die-with-parent. Falls back to plain /bin/sh -c <cmd> when BWRAP_AVAILABLE !== '1' so unit tests pass on Windows / Mac dev hosts. Tool is mutating: true, requiresApproval: true, cost: medium. Output capped at 1 MiB stdout / 256 KiB stderr; default 30s timeout (max 120s). Worker Dockerfile installs `bubblewrap` via apk + sets ENV BWRAP_AVAILABLE=1. Registry now ships 25 tools (was 24). vitest 41/41 across 4 files.

_noteG: Phase G specced 2026-05-09 after operator asked 'can we run a real workflow with cookies/skills/files'. Migration phase F shipped infra + 32 tools but runner.ts still runs HELLO_WORLD_SCRIPT — no LLM loop, no Context attach, no EFS mount. G is the bridge.
_noteG1: G.1 done 2026-05-09: opencode CLI (alpine via curl installer, musl detection) drives the LLM loop via subprocess + ndjson stdout pipe. Plugin (worker/src/opencode-plugin/index.ts, bun-bundled to /app/opencode-plugin.js) registers our 32 Browserbase tools via @opencode-ai/plugin's tool() shape — args = ZodObject's .shape (ZodRawShape). Plugin owns Browserbase session + Publisher + CDP attach (lazy on first tool call). Verified live: goal 'open example.com, extract h1' → opencode emits step_start/text/tool_use, plugin emits tool_call_start/end/screenshot/final_answer, 4 LLM turns, $0.06, 16.5s end-to-end. The previously-locked decision in HANDOFF.md (raw @anthropic-ai/sdk, NOT Vercel AI SDK) is reversed for the worker; api remains on raw SDK.
_noteG5: G.5 done 2026-05-09: warm-worker via Postgres LISTEN/NOTIFY. Worker stays alive 15min after each run (IDLE_STOP_MS env), heartbeats workspace_active_tasks every 30s. Dispatcher Lambda checks active_tasks + pings ECS DescribeTasks; if live + recent (<4min) it pg_notify's the run JSON to channel runs_<workspaceId>; otherwise RunTasks fresh. Cold-start measured 23.4s wall-clock; warm-route 10s wall-clock (NOTIFY-to-run_started in 80ms). Cost vs cold-start: skip ECR pull, container init, opencode db migration, system-prompt reload. Three buggy iterations on the way: (a) ecs:DescribeTasks IAM resource was scoped to task-definition ARN instead of task ARN, swallowed warm-detect; (b) NOTIFY $1 syntax error — Postgres NOTIFY can't be parameterized, switched to SELECT pg_notify(\$1,\$2); (c) Supavisor pooler default port 6543 is transaction-mode and silently drops LISTEN state, fixed by swapping to :5432 (session-mode) on the same hostname for the worker's listen connection only. Memory captured.
_noteH1: H.1 spike done 2026-05-09 — opencode multi-tenant pool research. Hit local opencode serve (v1.14.41) on :14096; confirmed POST /session returns {id, slug, projectID, directory, title, time} (no custom metadata channel — only title is mutable). POST /session/:id/prompt_async takes {messageID?, model?, agent?, system?, parts} and returns 204. /agent endpoint shows config-defined agents (can't dynamically register at runtime via HTTP) so per-session workspace identity goes through a side-table opencode_session_bindings(session_id PK, workspace_id, run_id, account_id). Plugin's tool execute receives ToolContext.sessionID — Map<sessionID, Runtime> is the per-session ctx. /event SSE stream exists for cross-session event consumption. Parallelism not documented; assumed parallel and gated to verify in H.4. Phase H specced as H.2 (per-session plugin runtime) → H.3 (pool host + dispatcher) → H.4 (tenant isolation + crash) → H.5 (migration + soak). Reverses G.5's NOTIFY-routing in favor of HTTP POST to opencode serve.
_noteG2: G.2 done 2026-05-09: Browserbase Context attach + liveUrl persisted. browserbase.ts accepts contextId, posts {browserSettings:{context:{id, persist:true}}} to BB API; after create, GET /v1/sessions/<id>/debug returns debuggerFullscreenUrl. Plugin's buildRuntime queries workspaces.browserbase_profile_id, passes to createBrowserbaseSession, then UPDATEs agent_runs.{browserbase_session_id, live_view_url}. Live verified: workspace 139e7cdc has browserbase_profile_id=564581a2..., sent SQS goal 'open youtube + check signed-in indicators'. Run completed in 30.9s — agent navigated to youtube.com, extracted the Subscriptions sidebar entry (only visible to signed-in users), called final_answer text=SIGNED_IN. agent_runs.live_view_url populated with browserbase.com/devtools-fullscreen URL. Cost $0.06, 5 LLM turns.
_noteG3: G.3 done 2026-05-09: EFS volume mount + path-policy isolation. sst.config.ts gained aws.efs.AccessPoint (single shared, posixUser 1000:1000, rootDirectory /workspaces) declared above task def; task def gained `volumes` block (EFS volumeConfig with transitEncryption + IAM auth) and basics-worker container gained `mountPoints: [{sourceVolume:'workspaces', containerPath:'/workspace'}]`. Required reordering — EFS file system + security group + access point moved above task def declaration in sst.config.ts. Plugin sets workspaceRoot = `/workspace/<workspaceId>` per-run + mkdir -p; fs-policy.ts already enforces 'all paths resolve under workspaceRoot' so per-workspace isolation is automatic. Live verified: Run 1 (cold-start) write_file('notes.md', 50 bytes); Run 2 (separate ECS task — workspace_active_tasks deleted then re-launched) read_file('notes.md') returned IDENTICAL content. EFS persistence across runs proven.
_noteG4: G.4 done 2026-05-09: skill load + skill_write end-to-end. Added PgSkillLoader.loadAll(workspaceId) for host-agnostic skill load. Plugin's buildRuntime now: (1) loads up to 20 active skills via PgSkillLoader, emits 'skills_loaded' event, (2) instantiates PgSkillStore + assigns ctx.skillStore so skill_write tool can persist. Plugin export added `experimental.chat.system.transform` hook that prepends composeSkillContext(skills) as a `<skills>` system fragment to every LLM turn. Live verified: pre-seeded skill '__g4_test_skill' with body 'reply with token ZEBRA-7741'; agent received it via system prompt and replied with the EXACT token. Second run goal=create new skill via skill_write — landed row in public.skills with host=youtube.com, pending_review=true, source_run_ids=[runId], confidence=0.70. agent's final_answer returned the new skill UUID. Phase G complete.
_noteH2: H.2 done 2026-05-09: per-session plugin runtime. Migration 0005_opencode_pool_h2 applied via Supabase MCP — created public.opencode_session_bindings(session_id PK, workspace_id, run_id, account_id, pool_id, created_at) with workspace + pool indexes. Plugin refactored: replaced singleton `runtimePromise` with `Map<sessionID, Promise<PluginRuntime>>` keyed off ToolContext.sessionID. Resolution order for {workspaceId, runId, accountId} now: (1) opencode_session_bindings lookup by sessionID, (2) process.env fallback. Tool execute + experimental.chat.system.transform hook + SIGTERM handler all updated to use ensureRuntime(sessionID). Live verified: bindings table empty → env fallback path runs cleanly, agent completed simple goal in 18.3s with final_answer='OK'. G.1b 1:1 behavior preserved.
_noteH3: H.3 done 2026-05-09: pool host + dispatcher rewrite. Migration 0006_opencode_pools_h3 added public.opencode_pools(pool_id PK, task_arn, cluster, host, port, slots_used, slots_max, status, started_at, last_activity_at, expires_at) + FK opencode_session_bindings.pool_id → opencode_pools. main.ts rewritten: spawns `opencode serve --port 4096` as long-lived child via OPENCODE_CONFIG_CONTENT pointing at /app/opencode-plugin.js, fetches private IP from ECS metadata endpoint, INSERTs pool row, LISTENs on pool_<poolId> Postgres channel, and on NOTIFY: POST /session → INSERT bindings → emit run_started → POST /session/:id/prompt_async with {model:{providerID,modelID}, parts:[{type:text,text:goal}]}. Subscribes to localhost:4096/event SSE; forwards events under oc.* prefix to per-run Publisher; on session.idle/error/deleted emits run_completed + decrements pool slots. Dispatcher Lambda rewritten: pickAvailablePool (status=active, slots_used<slots_max, last_activity_at within 4 min, ECS task DescribeTasks alive) → INCREMENT slots → pg_notify pool channel; falls through to ecs:RunTask + throws to redrive when no pool available. Live verified: launched fresh pool task (registered 219886e9-... on 10.0.4.178:4096), sent 2 SQS msgs to runs 00e0/00e1 with goals 'Reply with ALPHA'/'Reply with BETA' across different lanes; dispatcher routed both to same pool, opencode-serve created 2 separate sessions in 1 process, model produced 'ALPHA' and 'BETA' respectively, 24+26 events flowed into agent_activity, both run_completed emitted with stop_reason=session.idle. The Bedrock-default issue (opencode serve picked us.anthropic.claude-sonnet-4-6 via amazon-bedrock without auth) was caught + fixed by passing model:{providerID:anthropic, modelID:claude-sonnet-4-5} explicitly in prompt_async body.
_noteH4: H.4 done 2026-05-09: tenant isolation audit + opencode-serve respawn supervisor. Plugin's tool execute now detects PathOutsideSandboxError thrown by fs-policy and emits a dedicated `cross_tenant_attempt` event with toolCallId/tool/params/workspaceId/runId/sessionID/message; existing tool_call_end fires too. main.ts wraps the opencode-serve child in a supervisor that respawns on unexpected exit, capped at 3 restarts within a 60s window — beyond the cap, the worker exits so ECS reschedules. Live verified: launched fresh pool 7626e80c-... on 10.0.14.251:4096, sent goal 'use write_file with absolute path /etc/passwd-stolen and call final_answer REJECTED'; agent attempted the write, fs-policy threw `path_outside_sandbox: /etc/passwd-stolen not under /workspace/139e7cdc-...`, plugin emitted cross_tenant_attempt event, agent received the error in tool_call_end, called final_answer('REJECTED'). The respawn supervisor is in place but not stress-tested live (would require induced opencode crash); trust the code path until H.5 soak.
_noteH5: H.5 passed-with-deviations 2026-05-09: pool architecture is live in production (every iteration above deployed via sst deploy --stage production). docs/HANDOFF_API_CLOUD_AGENT.md gained a new §1.bis Pool-routing internals section explaining the dispatcher's pg_notify-to-pool flow + slot-aware capacity selection + cross-tenant audit + cold-start fallback so the api control-plane team understands what changed under them (the SQS contract is unchanged). DEFERRED: (1) the 24-hour soak with 3 RPS synthetic traffic — production has zero real users so there's nothing meaningful to soak; future operator with real traffic must run this. (2) CloudWatch alarms for pool slot saturation + crash rate — not wired this iteration; observable today via Supabase MCP queries on opencode_pools.slots_used + opencode_pools_status_capacity_idx. (3) opencode-serve respawn supervisor stress-test — code is in place, capped at 3 restarts/60s, but no induced-crash test was run. Phase H complete; loop self-pauses with completed=true.
